_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Idempotent `iw next-id` via `--idempotency-key` flag

steps.description: Add IdAllocation ORM model + Alembic migration for id_allocations table (composite PK (prefix, number), partial unique index on (prefix, idempotency_key) WHERE idempotency_key IS NOT NULL). Autogenerate misses postgresql_where on partial indexes — hand-edit the migration if needed. Commit the migration file (worktree-DB rule). Run make migration-check before reporting.
QV: Alembic migration round-trip + schema-drift check (immediately after Database step per orch/CLAUDE.md guidance)

TDD-RED first: write tests/unit/test_id_allocations.py covering AC1-AC4 + concurrent-INSERT race; confirm RED reasons are TypeError/AssertionError (not ImportError or collection error); then GREEN. Modify allocate_next_id() to accept keyword-only idempotency_key=None; add --idempotency-key Click option to next_id command; handle IntegrityError on the partial unique index by retrying SELECT (cap 3). Verify backwards compat: grep all callers, confirm orch/cli/batch_commands.py:326 still works unchanged.

Add tests/integration/test_idempotency_key_cli.py with at least three tests: CLI repeat with same key returns same ID (AC2 end-to-end); CLI without key still allocates fresh IDs (backwards-compat regression guard); CLI --json output identical on idempotent replay. Use testcontainer Postgres (NOT sqlite — partial unique index uses Postgres-specific WHERE syntax). Run only the new file before reporting.

Per-agent review of S01/S03/S04. CRITICAL on: missing postgresql_where on partial unique index (autogenerate gap); non-keyword-only idempotency_key (breaks batch_commands.py:326); INSERT not in same transaction as id_sequences increment; sqlite use instead of testcontainer; scope creep. HIGH on: missing IntegrityError retry; missing AC coverage in unit tests; missing no-key regression test in integration. Group by severity.
Apply fixes for CRITICAL/HIGH findings from S05. Re-run make migration-check if migration touched. Targeted test reruns only.

Cross-agent final review: independently re-run make migration-check + targeted unit+integration. Verify model↔migration parity (postgresql_where presence). Verify scope discipline via git diff --stat. Verify backwards compat at orch/cli/batch_commands.py:326. Verify CLI output bit-identical to today for the no-key path. Verify every CRITICAL/HIGH from S05 either fixed in S06 or contested with rationale.
Apply fixes for any CRITICAL/HIGH findings from S07. If S07 was clean, this step is a no-op — write a brief report and pass through.

Self-assessment of CR-00053 via the iw-item-analyze skill. Focus areas: (1) TDD RED evidence quality on S03; (2) whether S01 needed a fix-cycle to add postgresql_where to the partial unique index (autogenerate gap signal); (3) scope discipline vs the small allowed_paths list; (4) flakiness of the concurrent-INSERT race test; (5) cross-CR pattern vs CR-00052/CR-00051 for prompt-template improvements.
